english: up, to dig the soil; in arithmetic: *sum nyi lóg-pa ḍug** (*sum nyi log pa dug*) W. two times three are six.

pf. (b)slaṅs ((b)slangs), fut. (b)slaṅ ((b)slang), imp. sloṅ(s) (slong(s)), W. *laṅ-ce** (*lang ce*), I. causat. and transit. form to ldaṅ-ba (ldang ba). 1. to cause to rise, to help to rise, one lying on the ground; dgrá-ru sloṅ-ba (dgra ru slong ba) to cause a person to rise as an enemy (cf. dgrar ldaṅ-ba (dgrar ldang ba)), i.e. to make a person one's enemy Ś.g.; bsád-pai mi-ró sloṅ-ba (bsad pai mi ro slong ba) to resuscitate the slain; to excite, cause, inspire, compassion, fear, terror etc.; p̊rag-dog-gis, skyo-śas-kyis kun-nas bslaṅs-te (phrag dog gis, skyo shas kyis kun nas bslangs te) Glr., Mil. quite excited by envy and hatred, ni f.; esp. in pathology of the procatarctic or exciting causes of diseases: to kindle (a disease) into action, hence sloṅ-rkyén (slong rkyen) the exciting cause (of a disease); - to raise, to erect, a pile, post, wall Mil.; sláṅ-śiṅ (slang shing) a pile, stay, prop, erected or set up. - kun-sloṅ (kun slong) Lex., Mil.: nyon-moṅs-kyi sloṅ-kun-sloṅ (nyon mongs kyi slong kun slong) excitement (??). II. (perh. originally quite a different word), 1. to ask, require, ccdp. klu źig ṅá-la dpe sloṅ (klu zhig nga la dpe slong) a Lu asks me for the book Dzl.; bú-mo čuṅ-mar sloṅ-ba (bu mo cung mar slong ba) to ask a man's daughter in marriage Dzl.; esp. to beg, to try to get by begging: čuṅ-zad bsláṅ-ṅo (cung zad bslang ngo) we beg for a little of it! Dzl., p̊á-la sláṅs-nas kyer (pha la slangs nas kyer) he obtained it from his father by begging Mil.; bsód-snyoms sloṅ-ba (bsod snyoms slong ba) to collect alms by begging (sloṅ-ba (slong ba) partic. and sbst. beggar, mendicant Dzl, sloṅ-mk̊an (slong mkhan), sloṅ-ba-po (slong ba po) id.); hence. - 2. to collect, to gather, nor (nor) Cs. riches. - 3. to examine, to probe (a wound), rma-gsar mdzúb-mos (rma gsar mdzub mos) a fresh wound with the finger Thgy.; also: to search a man's house. - 4. to give, k̊a-lhág čig sdús-la sloṅ čig (kha lhag cig sdus la slong cig) gather some of the remnants of the meal, and give them to me! Mil., so in Sp. and C. frq.
alms, sloṅ-mo sloṅ-ba (slong mo slong ba) Lex., byéd-pa (byed pa) Cs. to ask alms, to beg; sloṅ-mos ots̊ó-ba (slong mos 'tsho ba) to live on alms; sloṅ-mo-pa (slong mo pa) beggar Pth.
1. Sch. to patch, to mend. - 2. Sch.: dpáṅ-po p̊yir slón-par byéd-pa (dpang po phyir slon par byed pa) to dissemble, to feign (?) - 3. Cs.: to thrust out.
the act of learning, study, slob ma myóṅ-ba (slob ma myong ba) to have had no instruction or education; slob k̊ríd-pa (slob khrid pa) to teach.

I. vb., pf. bslabs (bslabs), fut. bslab (bslab), imp. slob(s) (slob(s)), W. *láb-ce** (*lab ce*), to learn, to teach, ṅa (nga) or ṅá-la slob (nga la slob) I learn, ṅas slob (ngas slob) I teach, dé-la mk̊an slob γnyis-kyis lo-tstsa bslabs (de la mkhan slob gnyis kyis lo tstsa bslabs) both the abbot and the instructor taught him the art of translating Pth.; ṅa rtsis źig slob odód-pas slobs (nga rtsis zhig slob 'dod pas slobs) as I should like to learn something of mathematics, teach me! Pth.; bslábs-pas śes-te (bslabs pas shes te) when he had learned it Pth.; bsláb-čiṅ lóbs-pa yaṅ dka (bslab cing lobs pa yang dka) as learning is difficult, even if one is taught Dzl.; slób-tu ajúg-pa (slob tu 'jug pa) to let one take lessons, to have or get one instructed Dzl.; *t̊ú-gu-la gom-taṅ láb-ce** (*thu gu la gom tang lab ce*) to teach a little child to walk W.; mi-la yi-ge bslabs (mi la yi ge bslabs) schools were established Glr.; yón-tan slób-pa (yon tan slob pa) to teach (to learn) good, useful, things Pth. and frq. II. sbst. 1. the act of learning Dzl. - 2. teacher, instructor, brám-ze slób-pa (bram ze slob pa) a Brahman as instructor Dzl.; op̊ágs-pa slób-pa-rnams (phags pa slob pa rnams) the venerable preceptors (more than dge-sloṅ (dge slong), less than dgrá-bčom-pa (dgra bcom pa)) Tar. 5, 1. 31, 9. Comp. *lob-kyád** (*lob kyad*) W. use, practice, exercise. - slob-grwá (slob grwa) school, school-room, school-house; *lob-ḍa-k̊áṅ** (*lob da khang*) W. id. - slob-grógs (slob grogs) school-fellow, co-disciple. - slob-γnyér (slob gnyer) student, scholar, slob-γnyér gáṅ-du bgyis (slob gnyer gang du bgyis) where have you studied? at what college have you been a student? Mil. - slób-dpon (slob dpon) teacher, instructor, master, frq.; also a college-title like our bachelor etc.; 'the teacher' by way of eminence, is either Buddha or Pad-ma-byuṅ-γnás (pad ma byung gnas). - slob-abáṅs (slob 'bangs) scholar, pupil, disciple, = bu-slób (bu slob) Mil. - slób-ma (slob ma) id., frq. *lob-ló** (*lob lo*) report, rumour, fame W. - Cf. bslabs (bslabs).
exercise, practice, experience; mig-slobs ṅan-pa skye (mig slobs ngan pa skye) Mil. a bad custom of seeing begins to prevail (viz. that of looking downward, and minding only earthly things).
Mil., C. (W. *śaṅ** (*shang*)) the snow-leopard, nearly white, with small clusters of black spots; living on the higher mountains.
